questionHtml:
Which series is in order of increasing boiling point?

A.  CH2CH2CH3OH    CH3COCH3         CH3CH2CH3

B.  CH3CH2CH3         CH3COCH3          CH2CH2CH3OH

C.  CH3COCH3          CH2CH2CH3OH    CH3CH2CH3

D.  CH3CH2CH3         CH2CH2CH3OH    CH3COCH3

meta.title: Which series is in order of increasing boiling point? A. CH2CH2CH3OH CH3COCH3 CH3CH2CH3 B. CH3CH2CH3 CH3COCH3 CH2CH2CH3OH C. CH3COCH3 CH2CH2CH3OH CH3CH2CH3 D. CH3CH2CH3 CH2CH2CH3OH
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.2.9—Given comparable molar mass, the relative strengths of intermolecular forces are generally: London (dispersion) forces < dipole–dipole forces < hydrogen bonding. Explain the physical properties of covalent substances to include volatility, electrical conductivity and solubility in terms of their structure.
